title: hippocrepis salzmannii subsp. maura
intro: Hippocrepis salzmannii subsp. maura, also known as the spiny broom or golden lupin, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hippocrepis salzmannii subsp. maura include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as salves and creams for skin issues.
